title: vinca minor f. alba
intro: Vinca minor f. alba, also known as the white periwinkle or Madagascar periwinkle, is a flowering plant species within the Apocynaceae family, native to the Old World tropics and subtropics. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloids vincristine and vinblastine, which have been used to treat various cancers, including leukemia and lymphoma. The health benefits of Vinca minor f. alba include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in traditional medicine, and it can be found in various forms, including capsules, teas, and extracts.
